task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)

abstract.p: From R3.0.1.x onwards it is possible to start ADAM already when the printer is active (e.g. printing). Advantage: Waiting time can be reduced.

li.p: Analyze and report can start when the print job is not finished yet.
Entered data in the “Reason/Issues” is not lost, even when the system is restarted and can be finished later.
Restart of the engine in SDS mode is possible to avoid a restart of ADAM tooling.

note.p: When an MRE occurs while the system is in Service mode, it is allowed to access the normal user interface via “Service User Mode” and press Restart to recover the MRE.

li.p: There are two ways in which this restart can end:
When no SDS session has been started, the machine will start up normally after restart.
When an SDS session was started, the machine ends up in zero mode after restart. In order to proceed, to machine should be put in Cold or Warm mode by performing the associated SDS test.

After inserting the SAM the service icon can be touched and ADAM is started and can be used to enter and collect data (like work list errors and history), analyze errors, use the TSM and enter the System Service.
In the system service the status of the printer is visible, and additional options are available.
With the Service User Mode button you can return to the regular operator screen. The use of SDS is not allowed during printing.

When SDS is entered then the System Service menu will show greyed out options and no other tab is accessible nor return to the Service User Mode is possible until the printer has completed the running job.
In the training video under TSM Colorado 1630 " Introducing R3.0.1.x (Service version)" more details are available.
